stats.fact: Helicone acquired by Mintlify on March 3, 2026; team joined Mintlify in San Francisco
Helicone is in maintenance mode: only security patches, bug fixes, and new model support — no new features
Langfuse: 40,000+ builders, MIT-licensed, SDK-based (zero added latency vs proxy-based Helicone)
Langfuse pricing: Free (50K units/mo), Core $29/mo, Pro $199/mo; self-hosted ~$150/mo at 1M traces
LangSmith free: 5,000 traces/month; paid $39/seat/month plus $2.50-$5/1K traces; 1M traces/mo costs $2,500+
Braintrust free tier: 1M spans/month; eliminates per-seat pricing with unlimited users on all tiers
Langfuse Cloud at 1M traces/month costs ~$919 vs LangSmith's ~$2,500+ — 2.7x cheaper
Portkey supports 200+ LLM providers with routing, fallback, and semantic caching capabilities
Stockyard ships as a single ~25MB Go binary with embedded SQLite — no external dependencies or cloud component
LiteLLM security compromise in 2026 (alongside Helicone acquisition) created dual migration pressure in LLM proxy market

angles: Urgency angle: Helicone in maintenance mode — what migration timeline looks like and when to act
Proxy-based vs SDK-based observability: why the architectural choice matters for latency-sensitive apps
Cost comparison at scale: Langfuse self-hosted at $150/mo vs LangSmith at $2,500/mo for same 1M traces
LLM proxy ecosystem instability in 2026: Helicone acquired + LiteLLM compromised — is open-source safe?
Decision tree: which tool for which team (LangChain shops → LangSmith, eval-focused → Braintrust, self-host → Langfuse, multi-provider gateway → Portkey)
Vendor lock-in risk analysis: how to choose tools that minimize dependency on a single vendor
Self-hosted options deep dive: Langfuse OSS, Stockyard, and Arize Phoenix for data-sensitive teams
